prompt: A dimly lit master glovemaker's workshop, warm amber candlelight. A craftsman's worn wooden workbench dominates the center. One perfectly hand-stitched leather glove fits a single hand with uncanny precision — every knuckle crease, scar, and vein contoured flawlessly into the leather. On the table beside it, four other hands of different shapes attempt to wear identical gloves — each failing grotesquely, fingers bent wrong, leather pulling and splitting. Dramatic chiaroscuro lighting, ultra sharp, photorealistic, cinematic 4K photograph, wide composition. No text, no labels, no diagrams anywhere in the image.
metaphor: Glove perfectly molded to one hand's scars fits only that hand; fails on all others - exactly like overfitting memorizes training data but fails on new data.
